key_insight: GAIA learns geometry-conditioned neural operators that can handle forward, boundary value, and inverse PDE problems on arbitrary domains in a single architecture.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast surrogate modeling for simulation, reconstruction, and design optimization on complex geometries without retraining, iterative solvers, or graph construction.
method: Encodes boundary geometry and interior fields into geometry tokens.
Uses cross-attention to condition integral transform layers on local geometric features.
Unifies forward and inverse inference across different input/output domains in one pass.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on seven 2D and 3D benchmarks, including four new or extended inverse/BVP tasks, GAIA achieves state-of-the-art results on all inverse and BVP problems and remains competitive on forward tasks.
watch_item: Performance claims are benchmark-dependent, and stable deployment may still require careful handling of resolution shifts and unseen geometry distributions.
paper_title: GAIA: Geometry-Adaptive Operator Learning for Forward and Inverse Problems
